sanskrit: वायुर्यमोऽग्निर्वरुणः शशाङ्कः प्रजापतिस्त्वं प्रपितामहश्च। नमो नमस्तेऽस्तु सहस्रकृत्वः पुनश्च भूयोऽपि नमो नमस्ते॥
transliteration: väyur yamo ’gnir varuëaù çaçäìkaù prajäpatis tvaà prapitämahaç ca / namo namas te ’stu sahasra-kåtvaù punaç ca bhüyo ’pi namo namas te
literal_meaning: You are wind, death, fire, water, the moon; You are Prajāpati and the great-grandfather. A thousand times, again and yet again, homage to You!
contextual_meaning: Arjuna’s ego-boundaries dissolve; he perceives the entire cosmos as a single living Self, evoking awe, humility, and compulsive reverence.
psychological_state: Peak transpersonal experience bordering on ego-annihilation; compulsive repetition of homage as a defensive ritual against overwhelming expansion of self-boundaries.
trigger_scenario: A first-time psychedelic journey where the user feels identical with wind, oceans, and galaxies, then compulsively bows or prays to manage the intensity.
anchor_text: See the whole universe as one living Self and bow in wonder.
